commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4641c951ebb9300777b085cc15402a188985fa47)
commands.review_summary:
This PR expands the OpenCode Go provider catalog/docs/tests, changes onboarding to persist the static provider catalog, adds Qwen tiered pricing metadata, and commits a MiniMax proof screenshot.

PR surface: Source +71, Tests +108, Docs +12, Other 0. Total +191 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and the live OpenCode Go model endpoint support the catalog-drift report, and the PR diff touches the provider-owned catalog path. I did not run authenticated inference or a real onboarding flow.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is the maintainer choice between onboarding-persisted provider config and provider-owned prepared metadata, plus contributor real behavior proof that automation cannot supply.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): complete catalog, onboarding, and tiered pricing
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #91238.

This PR completes the OpenCode Go catalog repair initially attempted in #91351. The prior fix was incomplete — it only added Flat Pricing assertions in test files without implementing the official tiered pricing in `provider-catalog.ts`.

### Changes

- **New model entries** in `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts`:
  - `minimax-m3`: `anthropic-messages` API, 512K context (per OpenCode Go), $0.90/$3.60 (3× base pricing)
  - `qwen3.7-max`: `anthropic-messages` API, 1M context, $2.50/$7.50
  - `qwen3.7-plus`: `anthropic-messages` API, 1M context, $0.40/$1.60 (≤256K tier)
  - `hy3-preview`, `mimo-v2-omni`, `mimo-v2-pro` — additional bundled entries
  - `kimi-k2.7-code`: Added per official docs (`openai-completions`, 262K ctx, $0.95/$4.00/$0.19 cache)
- **API protocol fix**: `minimax-m2.7` and `qwen3.6-plus` corrected from `openai-completions` to `anthropic-messages`
- **Tiered pricing**: `qwen3.7-plus` and `qwen3.6-plus` now use proper split pricing at 256K tokens per [docs](https://opencode.ai/docs/zh-tw/go/)
- **Context fix**: `qwen3.6-plus` corrected from 262K → 1M context window
- **Pricing fixes** (5 corrections via automated verification): DeepSeek V4 Pro/Flash cacheRead (factor 10), MiMo V2.5/V2.5 Pro input/output/cacheRead, MiniMax M2.5 cacheRead
- **Pricing fix**: `minimax-m2.5` and `minimax-m2.7` `cacheWrite` corrected from $0 → proper values; added missing `cacheWrite: 0` to `minimax-m3`
- **Onboarding fix**: `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` now actually applies the bundled catalog
- **Merge resolution with upstream/main**: resolved 6 conflict regions in `provider-catalog.ts` and 4 in `index.test.ts`, merging tiered pricing + live discovery with new model entries
- **Tests and docs** updated to match the runtime catalog

## Linked context

Closes #91238

Related #91351

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Follows from live issue #91238 and ClawSweeper review on PR #91351. This is the full repair: adds all missing models, corrects API protocol, context windows, pricing, and onboarding.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `opencode-go/qwen3.7-max`, `opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus`, `opencode-go/minimax-m3`, and `opencode-go/kimi-k2.7-code` missing from bundled catalog — returning `model_not_found`. 5 pricing entries incorrect due to merge resolution artifacts. `minimax-m2.7` and `qwen3.6-plus` using wrong API protocol. `qwen3.6-plus` using wrong context window.
- Real environment tested: Windows arm64 local checkout from `origin/fix/opencode-go-catalog-missing-models` at `ba073fd95e`, Node v22, pnpm v11.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
pnpm test extensions/opencode-go
git diff --check
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
pnpm test extensions/opencode-go

> openclaw@ test:extensions /openclaw
> vitest run --config vitest-extensions.config.ts extensions/opencode-go

 ✓ extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts (12 tests) 115ms
 ✓ extensions/opencode-go/onboard.test.ts (1 test) 40ms
 ✓ extensions/opencode-go/register.test.ts (2 tests) 76ms
 ✓ extensions/opencode-go/opencode-go-app.test.ts (1 test) 42ms

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Start at  17:01:01
   Duration  485ms

git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: all 16 tests pass. Catalog now contains 19 model entries including `minimax-m3`, `qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `kimi-k2.7-code`. All pricing matches official OpenCode Go docs. `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` wires the catalog into provider config at init time.
- What was not tested: authenticated live inference through OpenCode Go and real gateway `models list` run. Live billing from upstream service not tested.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof validates the provider catalog, runtime registration, onboarding, and test assertion behavior from source on Windows arm64. macOS behavior expected to be identical.
- Before evidence: on `main` + PR #91351, the catalog was missing `minimax-m3`, `qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `kimi-k2.7-code`; `minimax-m2.7` and `qwen3.6-plus` used wrong API; `qwen3.6-plus` had wrong context; 5 pricing entries were incorrect; `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` returned config unchanged.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go`
- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go/onboard.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go/register.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go/opencode-go-app.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts` now covers all 19 catalog entries with `requireMapEntry` assertions, including `kimi-k2.7-code` (added), tiered pricing, API protocol, context window, and modality.
- `extensions/opencode-go/onboard.test.ts` expects 19 models.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- `opencode-go/qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `minimax-m3` returned `model_not_found` at runtime (#91238 logs).
- Incorrect API protocol on `minimax-m2.7` would cause transport errors.
- Incorrect context/pricing would cause wrong cost estimates.
- Missing `kimi-k2.7-code` caused `model_not_found`.
- `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` had no effect — onboarding was broken.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added and existing tests were updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. 7 new models now resolve in the catalog. 5 pricing corrections affect cost estimation. Onboarding now actually applies the catalog. API protocol corrections affect model routing.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Pricing metadata accuracy for all 19 models. The corrections match official OpenCode Go docs.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Every pricing value verified against official docs at https://opencode.ai/docs/zh-tw/go/. Tests assert the exact values.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Waiting for maintainer review and merge.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer review and CI/ClawSweeper.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addresses original issue #91238 (3 missing models) plus the additional drift found during merge resolution (5 pricing errors, missing `kimi-k2.7-code`, missing `cacheWrite`, missing docs rows).

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the code change and ran the validation above.
target.author: samson910022

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
